title: toxicodendron radicans var. pubens
intro: Toxicodendron radicans var. pubens, commonly known as the eastern poison ivy, is a plant species in the Anacardiaceae family that is native to eastern North America. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, due to its antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Toxicodendron radicans var. pubens include its ability to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and reduce inflammation. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into a salve or tincture to be applied topically to the affected area, and can also be consumed as a tea or capsule to reap its internal health benefits.
